methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Outpost Space is a seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Jason Dunn that has raised $40M+. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, Planet Labs, Impulse Space, and Apex Space.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable method given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($40M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for seed-to-next-stage advancement yields $100M. This method directly reflects growth expectations embedded in frontier-tech funding patterns. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied without disclosed revenue; the Space & Aerospace sector trades at a 25.8x median P/S across the 6-ticker public set, but revenue visibility on private targets is insufficient. Peer-median triangulation was not viable—while 11 same-sector peers exist, none met tight same-stage or adjacent-stage filters (fewer than 3 datapoints each), making sector-wide medians uninformative.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $85M to $115M, with a $100M median. This range reflects uncertainty around stage-progression step-up timing and the absence of granular peer-valuation anchors at seed stage in space technology. Key unknowns include revenue trajectory, capital efficiency, and near-term funding runway—standard opacities for early-stage aerospace ventures.
